_dataset_notice: Curated seed of national competitions. Every field was either confirmed against the competition's OFFICIAL website on the verification date or left as the literal 'VERIFY'. Never fill a VERIFY value from memory; cite an official source and update verification when confirming.

competitions.name: Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair (ISEF)
competitions.cost: Individual students do not pay to attend; their affiliated fair pays Society for Science affiliation fees ($825-$5,000 based on project allocation) plus per-attendee registration fees ($400-$425 per finalist/observer before April 30, 2026; $425-$450 after).
competitions.recognition: More than $7 million in awards, prizes and scholarships across 22 scientific categories.
competitions.location: ISEF 2027 finals in Los Angeles, CA (location rotates annually)

special_requirements.label: Qualify via an ISEF-affiliated fair
special_requirements.details: Finalists are selected by ISEF-affiliated regional, state, or national fairs; you cannot apply to ISEF directly.

competitions.description: Global final for the world's largest pre-college STEM research competition: roughly 1,700 finalists who qualify through ISEF-affiliated fairs in more than 60 countries present original research projects in 22 categories.

application_requirements.label: Find and register at a Society-affiliated regional/state/local science fair
application_requirements.details: ISEF cannot be entered directly; use the Affiliated Fair Network to find the fair serving your area.

application_requirements.label: Compete at your affiliated fair following its local rules and deadlines
Be selected as a finalist by your affiliated fair
application_requirements.details: Only finalists selected by an affiliated fair advance to ISEF; each fair sets its own project and paperwork deadlines.

application_requirements.label: Submit ISEF finalist paperwork via the Fair Dashboard within 12 calendar days of your fair
Attend and present at Regeneron ISEF
application_requirements.details: ISEF 2027 will be held May 8-14, 2027 in Los Angeles, CA; the affiliated fair pays the finalist's registration fee.

competitions.name: Regeneron Science Talent Search
competitions.cost: Free to enter; no application fee (confirmed by the absence of any fee across the official application-requirements, FAQ, and official-rules pages).
competitions.recognition: $3.1 million in awards distributed annually, including a $250,000 first-place prize; 2026 finalists received more than $1.8 million.
competitions.location: Application-based; Finals Week held in Washington, D.C.
competitions.description: The nation's oldest and most prestigious science research competition for high school seniors, who submit original independent research plus a full application (essays, a max 20-page paper, recommendations, transcripts) for holistic review.

application_requirements.label: Confirm you are a graduating high school senior meeting STS eligibility
Complete an original, independent research project
application_requirements.details: Research can be done at school, a research institution, in the field, or at home; often spans a year or more.

application_requirements.label: Prepare a research report (max 20 pages) plus required short essays and application sections
application_requirements.details: Sections include about you, your project, prior research projects, and your activities.

application_requirements.label: Obtain educator, project, and high school (transcript) recommendations
Disclose all support received for the research project and application
Submit the complete application online by the deadline
application_requirements.details: Application and all recommendations due November 5, 8PM ET, no exceptions. Test scores are optional.

competitions.name: AMC 10/12 (American Mathematics Competitions)
competitions.cost: School/venue pays registration per competition ($55 early bird by Sept 30, $75 regular by Oct 15, $115 late by Oct 28, 2026) plus $30 per 10-student license bundle; cost to individual students is set by their school
competitions.recognition: Top scorers are invited to the AIME (at least top 2.5% of AMC 10 scorers, top 5% of AMC 12 scorers), the pathway to USAJMO/USAMO qualification.
competitions.location: Administered in person at accredited schools, universities, math circles, and learning centers
competitions.competition_dates: AMC 10 A / AMC 12 A: November 5, 2026; AMC 10 B / AMC 12 B: November 13, 2026
competitions.description: 25-question, 75-minute multiple-choice mathematics competitions: the AMC 10 for students in grades 10 and below (under age 17.5), the AMC 12 for grades 12 and below (under 19.5), taken at registered schools and venues.

verification.notes: 2026-10-28 is the late deadline for returning competition managers; the regular deadline is 2026-10-15 (final for new competition managers).

application_requirements.label: School designates a Competition Manager who registers the school by the deadline
application_requirements.details: Regular registration deadline Oct 15, 2026 ($75 fee); late deadline Oct 28, 2026 ($115).

application_requirements.label: Competition Manager purchases student license bundles
application_requirements.details: $30 per bundle of 10 student licenses.

application_requirements.label: Competition Manager enrolls individual students on the registration platform
Student takes the 25-question, 75-minute exam on the scheduled competition date

competitions.name: Congressional App Challenge
competitions.host: U.S. House of Representatives (coordinated by the Internet Education Foundation)
competitions.cost: Free to enter; no registration or entry fee for students (confirmed by absence of any fee across the official rules, FAQ, and registration pages).
competitions.recognition: District winners' apps are eligible to be featured in the U.S. Capitol Building and on House.gov; winners are invited to the #HouseOfCode celebration and receive waived copyright registration application fees through the ARTS Act.
competitions.location: Online (judged district-by-district by Members of Congress)
competitions.competition_dates: Challenge launches in May; submissions close October 26, 2026; judging in November; winners announced in December
competitions.description: A district-by-district coding competition for U.S. middle and high school students, who create and submit an original app (any language, any platform) to their Member of Congress.

application_requirements.label: Create a student applicant profile in the registration portal
Identify and confirm your U.S. Representative's participation in the Challenge
Register as an individual or as a team of up to four students
application_requirements.details: All competing students must be U.S. residents at time of submission and enrolled in middle/high school.

application_requirements.label: Build an original app in any programming language, for any platform
Submit the app plus a 1-3 minute demonstration video, disclosing any AI tool use
Submit the completed entry online by the deadline
application_requirements.details: 2026 deadline: October 26, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

competitions.name: Congressional Art Competition (An Artistic Discovery)
competitions.host: U.S. House of Representatives / Congressional Institute
competitions.cost: No entry fee is stated; submission is through your U.S. Representative's office at no charge (confirmed on the official House.gov competition page).
competitions.recognition: Winning artwork from each district is displayed for one year at the U.S. Capitol; winners are recognized in their district and at an annual awards ceremony in Washington, DC.
competitions.location: District-based entry via each representative's office; winning works displayed in Washington, DC

special_requirements.label: Enter via your U.S. representative's office
special_requirements.details: Artwork is submitted to your district's Member of Congress by the deadline that office sets; there is no national registration.

competitions.description: A nationwide high school visual art competition, sponsored each spring by the Congressional Institute, in which students submit artwork to their U.S. representative's office and district panels select winners; over 650,000 students have participated since 1982.

application_requirements.label: Contact your U.S. Representative's office to confirm district participation and rules
Review the current year's Rules for Students and Teachers
Complete the Student Release Form
Submit the artwork and release form to your representative's office by their district's deadline

competitions.name: DECA International Career Development Conference (ICDC)
competitions.cost: DECA does not publish one fixed national per-student ICDC fee; registration is submitted and paid by the chartered association advisor, and attendees must book the official conference hotel for a minimum of four nights. Exact fees are set annually by each chartered association.
competitions.recognition: Competitive-event finalists from DECA's chartered associations compete to be named international champions.

special_requirements.label: DECA membership and association qualification
special_requirements.details: Open to DECA members who qualify through their chartered association's competitive events; registration is submitted by associations, not individuals.

competitions.description: The culmination of the DECA year, where more than 25,000 high school DECA members, advisors, and professionals gather and over 10,000 members who qualified through their chartered associations compete in business, marketing, finance, and hospitality events.

application_requirements.label: Become a DECA member through a chartered local chapter
Qualify for ICDC through your chartered association's competitive events/conference
Chapter advisor submits ICDC registration through the chartered association to DECA Inc.
Book the official DECA-assigned hotel property for a minimum of four nights
Attend ICDC and compete in your qualified event(s)

competitions.name: FBLA National Leadership Conference
competitions.cost: Registration and housing instructions vary by state; each chartered/state association distributes its own NLC Registration Guide and sets its own conference fees. Optional on-site certification exams at NLC cost $15-$55 per exam (2026 NLC).
competitions.recognition: Top-placing competitors in each competitive event are named to FBLA's National Top Ten List, recognized at NLC and shared via a national press release template.

special_requirements.label: FBLA membership and qualification
special_requirements.details: Attendees compete after advancing through FBLA's qualifying process (typically state conferences).

competitions.description: FBLA's annual national conference where the organization's top members convene to compete in leadership events, share successes, and attend workshops and exhibits; the 2026 Middle School & High School NLC was held in San Antonio, TX (June 29-July 2, 2026).

application_requirements.label: Join FBLA through a local chartered chapter
Qualify for NLC through your state's competitive events/state leadership conference
Register for NLC through your state association's NLC Registration Guide
application_requirements.details: Registration and housing instructions vary by state; guides are distributed each spring.

application_requirements.label: Attend NLC and compete in your qualified individual, team, or either-format event

competitions.name: NSDA National Speech & Debate Tournament
competitions.cost: $140 per student main-event entry; $280 for partner events; $30 per supplemental event; schools also post a $200 judge bond (2026 tournament rates; 15% discount when staying in the NSDA hotel block)
competitions.recognition: Competitors who advance through the week-long championship are named National Tournament Champions in their events.

special_requirements.label: Qualify via an NSDA district tournament
special_requirements.details: Entry to Nationals is earned through NSDA district tournament qualification, not open registration.

competitions.description: The premier national championship of the National Speech & Debate Association, where high school students who qualify through NSDA district tournaments compete in speech and debate events; a separate Middle School Nationals is also held.

application_requirements.label: Earn NSDA membership with at least the Merit degree to be district-eligible
Compete in and earn qualifying points at your NSDA district tournament
File a Single Entry Letter of Intent (SELI) declaring your qualifying event
application_requirements.details: No student may double-enter at the National Tournament.

application_requirements.label: School/coach completes National Tournament registration, payment, and judge obligations by the deadline
Attend and compete at the National Tournament
application_requirements.details: 2027 tournament: June 13-18, 2027, Phoenix, AZ.

competitions.name: Scholastic Art & Writing Awards
competitions.cost: $10 per individual entry, $30 per portfolio; fee waivers available via the online entry portal
competitions.recognition: Regional and national recognition via ceremonies, exhibitions, and publications; National Medalists (celebrated each June) are eligible for scholarships of up to $12,500.
competitions.location: Entered online and judged through regional affiliate programs; national medals awarded each June
competitions.competition_dates: Opens for entries in the fall; National Medalists recognized in June
competitions.description: Running since 1923, the Scholastic Awards recognize creative teens in grades 7-12 (ages 13 and up) across 29 categories of art and writing, with work submitted digitally and judged regionally then nationally.

application_requirements.label: Create a student (or educator) account in the Scholastic Awards online portal
Choose the category that best represents your work
Upload digital images/files of your work
application_requirements.details: Collaborative entries are not allowed in any category except Film & Animation, which permits up to 5 acknowledged collaborators.

application_requirements.label: Pay the entry fee or request a fee waiver
application_requirements.details: $10 per individual entry, $30 per portfolio; fee waivers available in the portal.

application_requirements.label: Work is judged through a regional affiliate program, then top entries advance to national judging
application_requirements.details: National Medalists are announced each June.

competitions.name: National History Day Contest
competitions.cost: NHD's National Contest itself does not charge individual students a fee (only a $65 adviser/teacher registration fee at Nationals); school, regional, and affiliate-level contests set and collect their own per-student fees, which vary by affiliate (official affiliate sites show roughly $15-$60 per student).
competitions.recognition: At the National Contest, the top three entries in each contest category receive awards, plus special prizes.
competitions.location: National Contest at the University of Maryland, College Park (after school, regional, and affiliate rounds)
competitions.competition_dates: National Contest: June 13-17, 2027; school/regional/affiliate contests earlier in the school year

special_requirements.label: Advance from school and affiliate contests
special_requirements.details: National Contest invitations go to top entries from school, regional, and affiliate rounds held earlier in the year.

competitions.description: A year-long historical research competition in which students in the Junior (grades 6-8) and Senior (grades 9-12) divisions create theme-based projects — documentary, exhibit, paper, performance, or website — and advance from school to regional/affiliate contests, with top entries invited to the National Contest.

application_requirements.label: Choose a project category (documentary, exhibit, paper, performance, or website) and a topic tied to the annual theme
Compete at your school-level contest
Advance to your regional/affiliate contest
application_requirements.details: Registration fees for these local rounds are set by each affiliate.

application_requirements.label: Top affiliate entries are invited to the National Contest
application_requirements.details: 2027 National Contest: June 13-17, 2027, University of Maryland, College Park.

competitions.name: Conrad Challenge
competitions.cost: Activation and Innovation Stage submission is free; teams that advance pay a $499 Innovation Stage team registration fee, and Summit attendees pay $499/guest (financial aid available for both).
competitions.recognition: Winning teams receive academic scholarships, an all-expenses-paid trip (presented by Equinor with United Airlines), free patent support from IP lawyers, and honors including Pete Conrad Scholar, Power Pitch Award, Citizenship Award, and Excellence in Education Award; exact scholarship dollar amounts are not published.
competitions.location: Houston, TX — Innovation Summit held on-site at Space Center Houston; the program is administered by the Conrad Foundation in partnership with Space Center Houston.
competitions.competition_dates: 2025-26 cycle (most recently confirmed): Activation Stage Aug 28-Oct 30, 2025; Innovation Stage Oct 31, 2025-Jan 8, 2026; Innovation Summit Apr 22-25, 2026. The 2026-27 cycle's dates are not yet announced.
competitions.description: The Conrad (Spirit of Innovation) Challenge, run by the Conrad Foundation (est. 2008 in honor of astronaut Pete Conrad), brings together a community of student innovators and entrepreneurs to develop viable solutions to global challenges.

verification.notes: Primary domain conradchallenge.org unreachable on 2026-07-06 and 2026-07-07; facts sourced from the official operator conrad.spacecenter.org (Space Center Houston). grade_levels/fields_of_study mapped from stated ages/categories. Re-check primary domain when restored.

application_requirements.label: Register a team of 2-5 students (ages 13-18) with an adult team coach (18+) via the Award Force portal
Complete the Activation Stage: form your team and define your innovation concept
Complete the Innovation Stage: develop and submit your solution materials, paying the $499 team registration fee
Advance as a finalist based on Innovation Stage judging
Attend the Innovation Summit in Houston, TX to pitch and compete for prizes
application_requirements.details: Summit registration is $499/guest; financial aid available.

competitions.name: Diamond Challenge
competitions.cost: Free to enter; Diamond Challenge provides free resources with no participation fee (confirmed on the official competition page).
competitions.recognition: More than $100,000 in awards; grand prizes in each track (Business Innovation and Social Innovation): $12,000 first, $8,000 second, $4,500 third, plus additional topical prizes.
competitions.location: Chase Center on the Riverfront, Wilmington, DE (Limitless World Summit); the program is run by Horn Entrepreneurship, University of Delaware, Newark, DE.
competitions.competition_dates: Submissions open September 16; submission deadline January 14 (5PM EST); finalists announced March 9; final summit April 29-30
competitions.description: A global entrepreneurship competition from the University of Delaware's Horn Entrepreneurship in which teams of 2-4 high school students (ages 14-18, with an adult advisor 21+) develop and pitch a business or social innovation concept for a share of over $100,000 in awards.

verification.notes: Official timeline shows a January 14 deadline without a year; presumed 2027-01-14 for the 2026-27 cycle and recorded as an estimate only.

application_requirements.label: Form a team (2-4 students, ages 14-18) with an adult advisor (21+) and develop a business or social innovation concept
Submit a written concept narrative (3-5 pages) and a 60-second introductory video by the submission deadline
application_requirements.details: 2027 deadline: January 14, 5PM EST.

application_requirements.label: Meet the minimum judging score to advance to the pitching round
Deliver a live 5-minute pitch (3-minute Q&A) or submit a pre-recorded 5-minute pitch video with a pitch deck
Finalists attend the Limitless World Summit in person to deliver final pitches and compete for prizes
application_requirements.details: Held April 29-30 at the Chase Center on the Riverfront, Wilmington, DE.

competitions.name: Profile in Courage Essay Contest
competitions.cost: Free to enter; no fee is mentioned on the official contest pages or the smapply submission portal.
competitions.recognition: First place: $10,000 cash award plus an invitation (with paid travel) to the Profile in Courage Award ceremony in Boston; second place: $3,000; three finalists: $1,000 each; fifteen honorable mentions; all participants receive a Certificate of Participation.
competitions.location: Online submission; winner honored at the JFK Presidential Library in Boston, MA
competitions.competition_dates: Annual; most recent deadline January 12, 2026; winners notified by April 30, ceremony in May
competitions.description: An annual contest in which US high school students write an original 700-1,000 word essay (with at least five cited sources) describing and analyzing an act of political courage by a US elected official who served during or after 1917.

verification.notes: Official site still shows the past 2026-01-12 deadline; the next cycle's date is not yet posted.

application_requirements.label: Review the contest topic and choose an eligible elected U.S. official (served during/after 1917, not JFK, not a president)
Research and write a 700-1,000 word essay analyzing a single act of political courage
application_requirements.details: Must cite at least 5 sources with a full bibliography; citations/bibliography don't count toward the word limit.

application_requirements.label: Have a nominating teacher (or, for homeschoolers, a parent/guardian) read the essay before submission
Submit the essay through the official online contest portal by the deadline
application_requirements.details: 2026 deadline was January 12, 2026 (11:59 PM ET); the next cycle's date is not yet posted.
